mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates synchronized demand and supply weakness, which triggers a risk-off repricing in equities and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or the combined proxy correlation drops below 0.3 while the predictive power vanishes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25 percent, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product expands corporate revenue bases and earnings expectations, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year directional accuracy drops below 50%, or forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not statistically higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 200,000 jobs signals robust labor demand that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where monthly payroll gains exceed 200,000, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates a shift from residential to commercial investment, which historically precedes equity market corrections due to sector rotation and margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict equity direction in out-of-sample testing, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below zero over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising unemployment acts as a lagging indicator that confirms the Fed's restrictive stance is successfully cooling the economy, which initially suppresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive in more than 55% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE rise simultaneously, or the joint signal's Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.
